goal: Add a side-effect-free operator dry-run planner for BPK-069 runtime patch application readiness, simulating application steps while keeping execution, patch application, server mutation, route mount, orchestrator, providers, DB, GitHub, writes, packages, workflows, and deployment out of scope.
scope_out: Do not apply patches, mount routes, call orchestrator, call providers, call DB, call GitHub, write files, deploy, change workflows, change packages, or enable execution.
claims: Runtime server patch application can be dry-run planned without applying it.
Ready BPK-069 readiness and operator dry-run refs are required.
Execution and patch application remain false.
assumptions: Actual runtime patch application requires a later task lock.
dod: Ready, missing dry-run ref, and blocked readiness cases are tested.
Focused test, typecheck, task-lock verify, and diff-check pass.
stop_conditions: Route mount, execution, orchestrator, provider, DB, GitHub, write, deployment, package, or workflow changes become necessary.
prior_task_findings: BPK-069 assessed runtime server patch application readiness without applying patches.
